title: Evangelion and Japanese Swords Exhibit
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion & Japanese Swords Exhibit             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Aug 18, 2012 2:53 pm http://www.crunchyroll.com/anime-news/2012/07/22-1/a-look-inside-japanese-sword-museums-evangelion-exhibit http://www.samurai-sword-shop.com/blog/evangelion-japanese-sword-exhibit-where-past-meets-future/ http://io9.com/5927993/master-japanese-craftsmen-make-real+life-versions-of-evangelion-weapons It's kind old news but I think that it's not been posted yet, at least searching the museum's name didn't give me any result, anyway from 14 July to 17 September the Bizen Japanese Sword Museum hosts the "Evangelion & Japanese Swords Exhibit". The replicas of the Spear and some of the Anima weapons seem really well done.

[]: Japanese STEEL STRONG             Quote Post by Fireball » Sun Aug 19, 2012 11:52 am The SoL is crazy.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sun Aug 19, 2012 1:05 pm that poster looks so fucking bad

[]: Re: Japanese STEEL STRONG             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Aug 19, 2012 8:16 pm Fireball wrote: The SoL is crazy.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Aug 19, 2012 8:36 pm "Crazy" is a good word for that exhibit. The blades look fine, but everything else . . . man. It's painful to see talent so painfully married to commercialized drek. The LoL's okay though. I guess it'd have to be one of the imitations, but it's still neat.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Aug 19, 2012 8:57 pm InstrumentalityOne wrote: that poster looks so fucking bad

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Aug 20, 2012 11:32 am Hmm, 1500 yen for a folder or whatever of Rei or Asuka holding a tanto... Doesn't look like the nodachi is that heavy either. Cool that they made the progressive katana from way back when.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Fri Jun 28, 2013 10:57 pm http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/gifu/news/20130627-OYT8T01297.htm

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Tue Jul 30, 2013 5:10 am soul.assassin wrote: http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/gifu/news/20130627-OYT8T01297.htm

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Oct 22, 2013 5:23 pm You ready for this Europe? Coming in 2014! Official website: http://www.eva-and-swords.com/ Watch out, Kaworu's armed so what will Shinji bring?

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Tue Nov 26, 2013 9:04 am Ended up checking this out today. It's on display for a month at the Ueno Park Royal Museum. Didn't plan on going but was walking pass, so what they hey, hey? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Nov 26, 2013 11:39 am Nice hamon in their LoL.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Nov 26, 2013 11:51 am The Spear of Longinus looks less like it has a hamon and more like it was pattern-welded, Viking-style, rather than Japanese-style. The Japanese used pattern welding themselves but didn't bring out the patterns quite as much as the Vikings did - especially since the Vikings could afford to use an acid wash to bring out the different colors from the different grades of steel. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pattern_welding The wiki article has some excellent pictures. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Katana_-_showing_alternating_layers.jpg Katana with pattern welding.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Nov 26, 2013 12:52 pm My fool ass needs to get to Japan. Won't make this exhibit though.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Nov 26, 2013 2:14 pm It's coming to Europe next year, though (no dates yet).

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Nov 28, 2013 9:47 am Evafan report blog: http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-2696.html The exhibit looks like it will stick around. Lower down the page there is concept art from Yamashita for a pointy metal thing that (from what I understand) will be brought into the physical world and displayed November 2014.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 28, 2013 10:33 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Evafan report blog: http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-2696.html The exhibit looks like it will stick around. Lower down the page there is concept art from Yamashita for a pointy metal thing that (from what I understand) will be brought into the physical world and displayed November 2014.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Nov 28, 2013 1:43 pm The "Rei" katana is pretty swag too in my opinion : SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Nov 29, 2013 7:57 pm Right now maybe keeping Shinji away from weapons with a reputation for ritual suicide might be a good idea.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Nov 30, 2013 6:33 am ^ Yeah, better keep him away from the Tantos! But still a nice katana that would have been nice! At least there is the Magorox Sword, which is "his" sword in ANIMA. Slightly off topic, but it's me, or the ANIMA adaptation is becoming more and more popular?

The replicas of the Spear and some of the Anima weapons seem really well done.
